strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Montana is a public flagship research university (R1) located in Missoula. It is highly accessible and known for its strong experiential learning, outdoor recreation integration, and top-tier programs in environmental studies, wildlife biology, and forestry.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Highly Accessible / Numbers-focused (Holistic for Honors College)

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university accepts roughly 96% of applicants, making it an accessible institution for a wide range of academic profiles.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students meeting minimum state requirements (2.5 GPA or 22 ACT/1120 SAT) are virtually guaranteed admission to the university, though specific honors or departmental programs may have higher thresholds.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Over 33% of the student body are first-generation college students, and there is a rapidly growing Native American student population.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The campus culture is highly supportive of non-traditional and first-generation learners, emphasizing inclusive prosperity.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The W.A. Franke College of Forestry and Conservation and the Wildlife Biology programs are heavily field-based, utilizing 25,000 acres of managed land including the Lubrecht Experimental Forest.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students interested in environmental sciences and natural resources receive unparalleled hands-on research opportunities rarely found at this scale.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Laid-back, outdoors-oriented, and deeply connected to the Missoula community. Strong emphasis on environmentalism, intramural sports, and Greek life.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Parking is frequently cited by students as a major issue on campus, particularly due to construction and limited overnight spaces.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: UM achieved Carnegie R1 (Very High Research Activity) designation, with research expenditures exceeding $150 million annually.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test Optional. Students may apply without submitting standardized test scores, but scores can be used to meet basic admission requirements or for course placement.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed. Yield rate calculated from 1,652 enrolled out of 9,180 admitted.

testing.policy_note: Only about 1% of admitted students submitted ACT scores, and ~0% submitted SAT scores.

colleges.name: College of Humanities and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking traditional liberal arts, sciences, and pre-professional tracks with a strong foundation in research.
colleges.student_archetype: Researchers, tech enthusiasts, and liberal arts scholars.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Requires M 171 (Calculus I) or M 162 (Applied Calculus) depending on concentration.
majors.notes: ABET-accredited B.S. program with concentrations in Software Engineering, Data Science, and Algorithm Development.

colleges.name: W.A. Franke College of Forestry and Conservation
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for outdoor-focused students pursuing careers in environmental stewardship, forestry, and wildlife management.

majors.name: Wildlife Biology
majors.special_requirements: Minimum cumulative GPA of 2.5 required to graduate. Requires minimum of 2 credits of experiential learning.
majors.notes: Offers terrestrial and aquatic concentrations. Heavily field-based utilizing Lubrecht Experimental Forest and Flathead Lake Biological Station.

colleges.name: College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students pursuing finance, accounting, and management with a desire for hands-on internships.

majors.name: Business Management
majors.special_requirements: Minimum GPA of 2.0 required in business courses.
majors.notes: Includes primary and secondary lower core requirements before progressing to upper-division major core.

application_deadlines.notes: Recommended fall deadline is November 1. Priority financial aid and scholarship deadline is March 1. Final deadline is one week before the term.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires short-answer responses and essays as a supplement to the UM application.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: General admission is primarily numbers-driven (2.5 GPA or 22 ACT or top half of class), but honors admission uses holistic review.

major_selection_tactics: Apply directly to the desired major, as general admission is highly accessible (96% admit rate) across most programs.
Apply by the December 1 priority deadline for Davidson Honors College to maximize merit scholarship consideration.
Submit applications early to secure optimal housing choices, despite the rolling admissions policy.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Not generally necessary for admission given the open accessibility, but students should enroll in pre-requisite tracks for selective professional programs (e.g., nursing or pharmacy) early.

scholarships.name: Western Undergraduate Exchange (WUE)
scholarships.benefits: Reduced out-of-state tuition for residents of WICHE states

scholarships.name: Presidential Leadership Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Top-tier merit award for Davidson Honors College admits

scholarships.name: Andrew and Wendy Davidson Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Support for Montana residents in business or health majors

scholarships.name: Montana University System Honor Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Waives tuition for eligible Montana high school graduates

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for AP exam scores of 3 or higher.
exceptions: Biology requires a 4+ for full sequence credit (BIOB 160/161N and 170/171N); a 3 grants partial/substitute credit.
ap_policy.usage: Credits awarded are assigned a grade of AP3, AP4, or AP5. Students with an AP Capstone Diploma receive priority consideration for Davidson Honors College.

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for Higher Level (HL) and some Standard Level (SL) exams with scores of 4 or higher.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Must complete a minimum of 30 credits at UM to earn a bachelor's degree. Transfer students must earn 39 upper-division credits and at least 30 from UM.

what_it_takes: 2.5 cumulative high school GPA or ACT composite of 22 or SAT combined of 1120.
Graduation from an accredited high school.
Completion of college prep curriculum (4 years English, 3 years Math).
essay_tips: Essays are not required for general admission, but are critical for the Davidson Honors College application.
Emphasize community impact, outdoor engagement, or leadership for Honors essays.
red_flags: Failing to submit required immunization records prior to enrollment.
Missing the priority deadlines for housing and financial aid.
insights: Students deeply appreciate the access to the surrounding Rocky Mountains and hands-on environmental research.
Parking is a consistent complaint among students living on or commuting to campus.
The campus culture is very friendly, inclusive, and unpretentious, reflecting the 'Montana Way.'
